title: rubus affinis subsp. relatus
intro: Rubus affinis subsp. relatus is a rare and lesser-known species of the Rubus genus, commonly referred to as a type of raspberry. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus affinis subsp. relatus include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
